title: Plan for immutability and safe upgrades
description: Decide deliberately between immutable contracts and an upgrade proxy, and protect upgrade authority and storage layout.
body: Use before deploying anything intended to evolve. Do not assume you can patch a deployed contract (code is immutable), and avoid an upgrade proxy without protecting its admin and storage layout. Actions: choose immutable-plus-redeploy for simple value, or a vetted proxy pattern (transparent/UUPS) for upgradeable logic; if upgradeable, guard the upgrade function with strong access control, preserve storage slot layout across versions, and use initializers instead of constructors. Validation signals include a storage-layout diff showing no slot collision and an upgrade test that preserves state. Risk boundary: a botched upgrade or unprotected proxy admin can brick the contract or hand it to an attacker; require review and a layout check before any upgrade.
